rationale: Title X better matches JICE style: concise, sentence case, clinically specific, and structured around incidence, clinical effects, and long-term prognosis. It gives readers a clearer map of the study endpoints than Title Y.
loser_weakness: Title Y is verbose, title-cased, and relies on the broad phrase “Clinical Impact.” The study-design tag adds length while contributing less topic-specific information than the endpoint list in Title X.
